title: Tennis Triumph: Svitolina's Stunning Comeback
story: Elina Svitolina's racket blazed with determination on October 28, 2018. The Ukrainian tennis star faced a tough challenge against American Sloane Stephens in Singapore. After losing the first set, Svitolina fought back fiercely. Her incredible rally led to a thrilling 3-6, 6-2, 6-2 victory in the WTA Finals title match. This remarkable comeback became the biggest win of Svitolina's career, showcasing her skill and never-give-up spirit.

title: Racing Legend Fangio Speeds to Victory
story: Vroom! The roar of engines filled the air as Juan Manuel Fangio zoomed across the finish line. title: Hockey Legend's Final Goal
story: Skates flashed across the ice as Bobby Orr, one of hockey's greatest players, scored his last NHL goal on October 28, 1978. title: Prime Number Prodigy's Birthday
story: A math whiz was born on October 28, 1960, in Walnut Creek, California. Landon Curt Noll grew up to become a computer scientist and mathematician with a knack for finding huge prime numbers. story: Imagine rock stars with superpowers! That's what happened when the band KISS starred in their first TV movie. On October 28, 1978, NBC aired 'KISS Meets The Phantom of the Park.' title: Tchaikovsky's Final Symphony Debut
story: The concert hall buzzed with excitement. Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky, the famous Russian composer, raised his baton. On October 28, 1893, he conducted the first-ever performance of his Symphony Number Six in B minor. Known as 'Pathetique', this powerful piece moved the audience in St. Petersburg. Sadly, it was Tchaikovsky's last symphony. He passed away just nine days after this memorable premiere.

title: The Musical Wizard of Electronic Sound
story: Gershon Kingsley, a musical wizard who helped create electronic music, was born in 1922. He played a special instrument called the Moog synthesizer, which made futuristic sounds.
